verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a single-pass type 1 transmembrane protein resident in the ER membrane that redistributes to ER-PM junctions upon Ca2+ store depletion to gate Orai1/CRAC channels. Importantly, STIM1 has a documented extracellular N-terminal domain (EF-hand and SAM domains facing the ER lumen / extracellular space at the PM). Surface biotinylation and antibody studies confirm a pool of STIM1 at the plasma membrane under basal and stimulated conditions, with the N-terminus accessible extracellularly. This stable TM topology at the PM with an exposed ectodomain qualifies it as a surface-accessible target.
